programs.name: Hoosier Care Connect
programs.what_it_does: Provides health insurance coverage to medically and financially needy seniors and disabled individuals through Indiana's Medicaid program. It covers long-term care services including home-based options to help recipients remain in the community.[3][9]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+, blind, or disabled individuals with low income and assets (e.g., $2,000 individual/$3,000 couple resources limit).[3]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, and long-term services like attendant care and home-delivered meals.[3]

programs.name: Aged and Disabled Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services as an alternative to nursing home placement. Services include adult day health care, assisted living, attendant care, case management, home-delivered meals, and personal emergency response systems.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and disabled adults aged 65+ who meet nursing facility level of care and Medicaid financial eligibility.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Covers specific waiver services up to assessed needs; prevents institutionalization with personalized home care support.[3]

programs.name: Program for All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings, including adult day health care and home care. It coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid benefits for participants.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified for nursing facility level of care who can live safely in the community with PACE services.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of all needed health and long-term care services at no cost to eligible dual-eligible participants.

programs.name: Healthy Indiana Plan Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and co-insurance for low-income seniors via Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI) programs. Administered through Indiana Medicaid.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries with limited income/assets (e.g., QMB: ~$1,255/month individual income limit in 2026; higher for SLMB/QI).[3]
programs.benefit_value: QMB covers full Part A/B costs (~$185 Part B premium + deductibles); SLMB pays Part B premium; QI pays Part B premium for highest eligible.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to purchase nutritious food at approved retailers. Helps low-income seniors afford groceries and combat food insecurity.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income below ~130% federal poverty level (~$1,951/month individual in 2026); higher asset limits for elderly/disabled.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-$300/month per eligible senior household; max benefits up to ~$291/individual (2026 rates vary by income).

programs.name: Energy Assistance Program (EAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help pay heating and cooling bills during winter and non-winter months. Provides one-time payments directly to utility providers.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors 60+ with income ≤150% poverty (~$2,325/month individual); priority for elderly/disabled.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Average $400-$600 grant per household per heating season; additional crisis grants up to $1,000.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to owner-occupied homes. Reduces energy costs and improves safety/comfort.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters including seniors 60+ at or below 200% poverty; priority for elderly, disabled, and high energy users.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Home improvements valued at $5,000-$10,000+ per household; lifetime energy savings of 20-30%.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare options, including Parts A/B/D, Medigap, and Medicare Advantage. Helps seniors select and appeal coverage issues.[1]
programs.who_its_for: All Indiana Medicare beneficiaries, especially those 65+ navigating complex insurance choices.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Personalized guidance preventing costly errors; average savings $500+/year through optimal plan selection.

programs.name: Home-Delivered Meals (via Aging & Disability Resource Centers)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Often includes safety wellness checks by delivery staff.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or with mobility impairments.[4]
programs.benefit_value: 5-7 meals/week at no/low cost (suggested $3/donation); serves 1M+ meals annually statewide.

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, training, counseling, and supplies to family caregivers of seniors. Offers breaks from caregiving duties through paid services.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of adults 60+ or grandparents raising children; income-based eligibility.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $2,000/year; training and support preventing caregiver burnout.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid community service work training leading to unsubsidized employment. Provides minimum wage jobs at nonprofits for skill-building.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ (income ≤125% poverty); priority for veterans, qualified spouses, older individuals.[1]
programs.benefit_value: 20 hours/week at federal/state minimum wage (~$7.25-$10/hour); work experience + employment placement.

programs.name: Indiana Legal Services (Senior Legal Aid)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free civil legal assistance for issues like benefits, housing, consumer problems, and elder abuse. Includes advice, representation, and self-help resources.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (≤125% poverty) unable to afford private attorneys.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Full-scope representation recovering thousands in benefits/housing; statewide senior hotline.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care facilities. Investigates complaints and resolves issues confidentially.[4]
programs.who_its_for: All residents/families in Indiana long-term care facilities; services free and independent.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Resolves 80%+ of 5,000+ annual complaints; protects rights and improves care quality.

programs.name: Indiana's Senior Property Tax Deduction
programs.what_it_does: Reduces property tax burdens for eligible seniors, making homeownership more affordable.[1] This is a state-funded initiative specific to Indiana.
programs.who_its_for: Eligible Indiana seniors who own property.[1]
programs.whats_unique: This is a state-specific property tax relief program not universally available across all states.[1]

programs.name: Structured Family Caregiving
programs.what_it_does: Provides financial stipends and comprehensive support services to family members who care for elderly relatives at home.[1] This innovative Indiana program recognizes the role of family caregivers in maintaining senior health and welfare.
programs.who_its_for: Family members caring for elderly relatives at home in Indiana.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Indiana's approach combines both financial compensation and guidance resources specifically designed for family caregivers, fostering aging in place within family settings.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Stipend amount not specified in search results

programs.name: Community and Home Options to Institutional Care for the Elderly and Disabled (CHOICE)
programs.what_it_does: Offers services that help seniors stay in their homes or communities as long as possible rather than moving to institutional care.[1] This waiver program provides home and community-based services.
programs.who_its_for: Eligible Indiana seniors and disabled individuals.[2]
programs.whats_unique: CHOICE is Indiana's home and community-based waiver program with income limits (monthly income limit of $2,901 for eligible individuals).[2]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by services provided; not specified in search results

programs.name: Indiana PathWays for Aging
programs.what_it_does: The new Aged & Disabled Waiver specifically for Hoosiers age 60 and older, providing home and community-based services through a care and service coordinator.[3] Beneficiaries receive the same services as the previous Aged and Disabled Waiver program.
programs.who_its_for: Indiana residents age 60 and older who meet eligibility criteria.[3]
programs.whats_unique: This is Indiana's updated version of the Aged & Disabled Waiver, restructured to serve seniors 60+ with coordinated care services.[3]
